etymology.description: 星(星) (xīng, "star") + 期(期) (qī, "period; phase; date") = a time period marked by celestial objects -> week, as the traditional Chinese calendar aligned with cycles of celestial bodies like stars and planets.
etymology.interpretation: The sense of 'week' comes from the concept of the 'seven-day planetary week,' where each day corresponded to one of seven celestial bodies: the sun, the moon, and five planets. 星(星) (xīng, "star") relates to the astronomical observation aspect, while 期(期) (qī, "period; phase; date") captures the cyclic time unit aspect, creating a concept of regular celestial and temporal cycles marking the division of time into weeks or weekday categories.

examples.chinese: 这个星期我很忙(這個星期我很忙)。
examples.english: I am very busy this week.

examples.chinese: 星期一是他最喜欢的一天(星期一是他最喜歡的一天)。
examples.pinyin: Xīngqī yī shì tā zuì xǐhuān de yītiān.

examples.chinese: 我们每个星期去游泳(我們每個星期去游泳)。
examples.pinyin: Wǒmen měi gè xīngqī qù yóuyǒng.
